marketMood: Risk-off: AI semiconductor sell-off deepens on BofA rate hike bets and Broadcom overhang, while Micron earnings tonight and Fed stress tests at 4 PM EDT are the day's two binary catalysts.

upcomingEvents.event: Fed 2026 Bank Stress Test Results (4 PM EDT)
Micron Q3 FY2026 Earnings After Close
US May New Home Sales Data
US May PCE Inflation Data + Q1 GDP Final Estimate

topStories.headline: $1.3 Trillion Wiped from Global Chip Stocks in AI Sell-Off
topStories.summary: A hawkish BofA rate hike note and collapse in South Korean memory stocks — SK Hynix and Samsung each -12%+ — triggered a two-day global semiconductor rout, dragging Micron -13%, Marvell -8%, SanDisk -11%, and the Nasdaq -2.21%.
topStories.marketImpact: Semis/tech ↓ globally; Kospi -10% (worst day since 2020); defensives outperform — real estate ↑ 1.38%, energy ↑ 1.24%; flight to Treasuries presses US 10Y to 4.48%

topStories.headline: SpaceX Prices $25B Inaugural Bond Sale Days After Record IPO, Stock Sinks 16%
topStories.summary: Days after its record IPO, SpaceX (SPCX) launched a 5-tranche investment-grade bond deal to refinance its $20B bridge loan; the fundraising scale raised investor concerns about capital intensity despite a disclosed $100B+ cash pile.
topStories.marketImpact: SPCX ↓ 16.4% — steepest single-day decline since IPO; broader space/tech IPO sentiment watch; bond markets absorbing large new supply

topStories.headline: US-Iran 'Roadmap' Agreed; Washington Grants 60-Day Iran Oil Sales License
topStories.summary: JD Vance confirmed a US-Iran roadmap toward a final deal on June 21, following the June 17 Islamabad MoU ceasefire framework; the US simultaneously issued a 60-day license for Iran to sell oil internationally, with 30M+ barrels shipped last week.
topStories.marketImpact: WTI crude ↓ to ~$73.4/barrel (multi-month low); energy sector watch; geopolitical risk premium easing; Strait of Hormuz traffic recovering

topStories.headline: Micron Reports Q3 FY2026 After Close Tonight — HBM4 and 2027 Capacity in Focus
topStories.summary: Street expects $19.72 EPS on $34.52B revenue — a 942% YoY EPS surge — with gross margins near 81% and full HBM supply sold out; HBM4 capacity guidance and 2027 customer commitments are the key read-through for the AI memory cycle.
topStories.marketImpact: MU ↑↓ binary event after close; proxy read-through for NVDA, AMD, and entire AI memory complex; context: MU already -13% on Monday ahead of print

topStories.headline: Fed Rate Hike Probability Surges to 68% for September After BofA Note
topStories.summary: After the June 17 FOMC lifted core PCE projections to 3.3% and held rates at 3.50–3.75%, BofA published a note flagging a September hike as increasingly credible, amplifying the chip sell-off and pushing DXY to ~101.4.
topStories.marketImpact: Growth stocks ↓; USD ↑ (DXY ~101.4, 13-month high); USD/JPY hit 161.58 — yen near weakest since 1986; rate-sensitive sectors under pressure across the board

topStories.headline: Fed Releases 2026 Annual Bank Stress Test Results for 32 Major Lenders at 4 PM EDT
topStories.summary: The Fed releases results for 32 large US banks tested against a severe scenario including 10% unemployment, 30% home price decline, and 39% CRE drop; results will not affect capital requirements until 2027 as the Fed reviews its stress-testing models.
topStories.marketImpact: Major bank stocks ↑ on clean bill of health; buyback authorization signals key post-4 PM catalyst; JPM, BAC, C, WFC, GS, MS all in focus

companiesToWatch.name: Micron Technology
companiesToWatch.reason: Q3 FY2026 earnings after close; $19.72 EPS consensus after Monday's 13% single-day drop — binary event for the entire AI memory complex tonight

companiesToWatch.name: SpaceX
companiesToWatch.reason: Fell 16.4% on its inaugural $25B bond offering; watch for continued de-risking or dip-buying given strong Starlink and launch fundamentals

companiesToWatch.name: JPMorgan Chase
companiesToWatch.reason: Among 32 banks facing stress test results at 4 PM EDT; a clean result could unlock buyback authorizations and lift sector sentiment

companiesToWatch.name: Nvidia
companiesToWatch.reason: No fundamental catalyst for Monday's decline — caught in the semiconductor rout; Micron guidance tonight is the key read-through for AI demand

companiesToWatch.name: Broadcom
companiesToWatch.reason: Original catalyst of the sell-off cycle after Q2 AI revenue guidance disappointed on June 4; market uses AVGO as the lens for AI capex sustainability

latestPicks.company: X-Fab Silicon Foundries SE (Euronext Paris: XFAB FP)
latestPicks.thesis: Sole high-volume SiC foundry with silicon photonics/CPO capacity; dual CHIPS Act funded — Serenity's May 27 pick described as 'next TSEM' positioned for the 2027 co-packaged optics volume ramp
EML (electro-absorption modulated laser) chokepoint — single-supplier to hyperscaler AI photonics buildout; part of core supply-chain map
InP (indium phosphide) substrate manufacturer; thesis validated when IntelliEPI CEO confirmed InP shortage in Q1 2026, a year after Serenity's call
Silicon photonics foundry chokepoint — one of few fabs with SiPh capacity for CPO-era transceivers

priorPickMoves.move: Rallied sharply after Serenity's May 27 post; Bloomberg covered the retail-driven surge with market cap expanding from ~$1.28B to ~$1.7B; now under pressure in the broader semi sell-off
Down 13% Monday in the broad AI semiconductor sell-off — upstream in Serenity's supply-chain map; earnings tonight are the catalyst to watch

serenity.sentiment: Track record widely praised — self-reported +4,502% YTD as of June 2026 (per Lookonchain). Current semiconductor sell-off generating community debate on whether AI capex has peaked. Persistent pushback on returns being unaudited and leveraged. Chinese financial media coverage (EastMoney, TechFlow) remains elevated. No new posts found in past 1–3 days — check X directly: https://x.com/aleabitoreddit

tickerDescriptions.MU: Micron Technology, memory chip and HBM (high-bandwidth memory) manufacturer central to AI infrastructure
tickerDescriptions.AVGO: Broadcom Inc., diversified semiconductor and infrastructure software company with a major AI custom ASIC business
tickerDescriptions.NVDA: Nvidia Corporation, the dominant designer of AI GPUs and accelerators
tickerDescriptions.AMD: Advanced Micro Devices, maker of CPUs and AI accelerators competing with Nvidia in the data center
tickerDescriptions.MRVL: Marvell Technology Group, networking and custom AI ASIC semiconductor company
tickerDescriptions.SNDK: SanDisk, NAND flash memory manufacturer spun out from Western Digital
tickerDescriptions.SPCX: SpaceX, private aerospace, Starlink satellite internet, and launch services company that recently completed its record IPO
tickerDescriptions.SPY: SPDR S&P 500 ETF tracking the 500 largest US publicly traded companies
tickerDescriptions.QQQ: Invesco QQQ ETF tracking the Nasdaq-100 index of the largest non-financial Nasdaq-listed companies
tickerDescriptions.TLT: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF, a proxy for long-duration US government bond prices
tickerDescriptions.JPM: JPMorgan Chase, the largest US bank by assets
tickerDescriptions.BAC: Bank of America, major US commercial and investment bank
tickerDescriptions.C: Citigroup, global US bank with significant international operations
tickerDescriptions.WFC: Wells Fargo, major US retail and commercial bank
tickerDescriptions.GS: Goldman Sachs, premier US investment bank and financial services firm
tickerDescriptions.MS: Morgan Stanley, major US investment bank and wealth management firm
tickerDescriptions.XOM: Exxon Mobil Corporation, the largest US integrated oil and gas major
tickerDescriptions.XLE: Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF tracking US energy companies including oil majors and E&P firms
tickerDescriptions.USO: United States Oil Fund ETF tracking front-month WTI crude oil futures
tickerDescriptions.LITE: Lumentum Holdings, maker of EML lasers and photonic components for datacom and AI infrastructure
tickerDescriptions.AXTI: AXT Inc., producer of compound semiconductor substrates including InP used in AI photonics
tickerDescriptions.TSEM: Tower Semiconductor, a specialty analog semiconductor foundry with silicon photonics capabilities

quickHits: Paychex (PAYX) and Jefferies Financial (JEF) also report earnings today — proxy reads on US labor markets and financial sector health (Reuters)
US May new home sales data due today — real-time gauge of rate sensitivity in housing with 30-year mortgage rates elevated (Bloomberg)
USD/JPY hit 161.58, near weakest yen since 1986 — Japan Finance Minister in contact with US Treasury's Bessent; BoJ intervention risk elevated (Reuters)
BofA note flagged sticky core PCE (3.3% vs 2% target) as key driver of rate hike bets — Friday's May PCE print is the week's most-watched data point (FXStreet)
